description: A cognitive psychology teacher has run 20 university students through a computerized parity judgment task to demonstrate the SNARC effect. Analyze the data at ~/pebl/data/snarc_data.csv to compute the SNARC regression slope for each valid participant. The SNARC slope is the unstandardized beta coefficient from a simple linear regression predicting dRT (mean_RT_Right - mean_RT_Left) from the number magnitude (1-9). Exclude participant sub-99 and all block 1 trials. For RT calculations, use only correct trials (correct=1) where 200 <= rt_ms <= 1500. Calculate 'overall_accuracy' using all block 2 and 3 trials before RT filtering. Output a report to ~/pebl/analysis/snarc_report.json containing a 'participants' list (with 'id', 'overall_accuracy', 'mean_rt_ms', and 'snarc_slope', or 'excluded': true and a 'reason' for sub-99), plus 'group_mean_snarc_slope' and 'group_mean_accuracy' representing averages across valid participants.
